name: enrich_fk_helpers
details: enriches fk helper columns
configuration_input: Frame schema metata: Fk columns must be marked as such and carry a relation to the reference target.

name: drop_helpers
details: drops the fk helper columns
configuration_input: data written by the projection especially which column is a helper

name: helper_config
details: applies a heuristic to derive configuration for fk helpers
configuration_input: conventions that mark PK and FK fields, e..g ID, ID(<referenced_table>)

name: compact_codec
details: compact multiple values / columns to one
configuration_input: separators and various options for compacting, allowed codes

name: expand_codec
details: expand one column to multiple values
configuration_input: Data written by the projection especially separators and compacting strategy

name: xref_crosstable
details: project a tuple relation (xref) to a pivot like projection (crosstable)
configuration_input: entity for both vertical and horizontal column

name: crosstable_xref
details: write the crosstable edits back to the xref representation
configuration_input: data written by the projection especially which column is a helper

name: expand_multiaxis
details: combines a sequence of projections that construct a multiaxis crosstable projection. Depending on the config some of these blocks may not be executed. (cell-codec / xref_crosstable / enrich headers)

name: compact_multiaxis
details: combines a sequence of inverse projections that deconstruct a multiaxis crosstable

name: add_legend
details: adds a legend to a presentation, depending on configuration restricting values in the target cells. Entries can be taken from config or other frames

name: drop_legend
details: drops a previously added legend

name: add_table
details: add a table and create appropriate metadata

name: remove_table
details: remove a table and delete appropriate metadata

name: rename_table
details: rename a table and keep metadata consistent

name: rename_key
details: rename a PK and keep all refeences consistent

name: validate_integrity
details: prevent dangling references, ensure pk uniques.

name: extend_xref_existence_attribute
details: if a crosstable is not supposed to carry information just mark the relation of the enities, this transformation can acs a pseudo “value” to a pair of values creating the trippel needed for xref_crosstable
configuration_input: The “value” with which the relation is noted in the crosstabel “X”, “OK”, “TRUE”, “1”

name: drop_xref_existence_attribute
details: to create a pair the third value of the tripple is dropped, which means user captured values are lost, just their existence is carried allong

name: adjust_presentation_names
details: Presentation names of tables and columns can be adjusted. Either by explicitly providing names to be used in spreadsheet presentation, that differ from the filenames / json field names or by applying a policy (perhaps configurable), e.g. transform _ in names to widespaces, capitalize first letters etc.

name: query_replace
details: Influnece data directly (e.g. to change “” to “n.a.” for a certain column when changing from structured data to presentation data) the opposite direction can be relalised by an  adjusted query-replace but round trip integrity is user repsonsibliity.

name: bootstrap
details: a generic way to enter any metadata to the pipeline. There is a way to differentiate between the intent to overwrite existing meta or to provide a default only for missing meta.

name: prune
details: a way to delete  data from meta – used to clear information that is not relevant beyond the scope of the pipeline at its end to prevent accumulation of meta.

name: split_by_discriminator
details: splits a table by the value of one coumn into separate shards. Can be used for storage sharding (useful to avoid mege conflicts) or to  create similar xrefs of 2 entities for each of the value – in this case the respective column acts as a third dimension of the formerly 2d cross table

name: merge_by_discriminator
details: merge a set of tables by discriminator

name: merge_list_to_separated
details: read a list value (as it occures in structured data like json) and convert into one merged value that contains a list of strings separated by a configurable separator. Provide an option to process list of pairs by using 2 separators (“key1” , “value1” / “key2” , “value2”)

name: split_list_to_separated
details: transform the list of separated strings back to the list form so it can be stored in json as a nested list

name: merge_list_to_separated
details: read a column that contains n values separated by a configurable separator and asumming the resulting strings are foreign keys for another table, construct an xref  between this entity and the entity refrenced by the foreign key (tupple representation). Either use a pair of both entities or use a tripple if the separated values are not only the keys but keys with an additional string.

name: split_list_to_separated
details: take an xref and resolve the relation back to one (or both) of the entitiy talbes involved representing the opposing relation to a  separated list.

name: flatten_object_to_column
details: read a column that contains a string serialization of an object and deserialize it so it can be wirtten as a nested json.

name: unflatten_column_to_object
details: read a column that contains an object and serialize it as a string that is stored in one column so it can be represented in a spreadsheet
